response: To arrange three indistinguishable rooks on a 6 by 6 board without any two attacking each other, we must first select three distinct rows and three distinct columns. There are 6 rows available, so the number of ways to choose 3 rows is the combination of 6 taken 3 at a time, which equals 20. Similarly, there are 6 columns available, and the number of ways to choose 3 columns is also the combination of 6 taken 3 at a time, which equals 20. Once the three rows and three columns are selected, they form a 3 by 3 subgrid where we must place the rooks. In this subgrid, each rook must occupy a unique row and a unique column. Since the rooks are indistinguishable, the number of ways to place them corresponds to the number of ways to match the three chosen rows to the three chosen columns, which is the number of permutations of 3 items. The number of permutations of 3 items is 6. Therefore, the total number of arrangements is the product of the number of ways to choose the rows, the number of ways to choose the columns, and the number of ways to arrange the rooks within the selected intersection. Multiplying these values together gives 20 times 20 times 6. The final result is 2400.
